title: Power tool with housing part retainer
abstract: A power tool comprises a housing (10) with a main part (A) supporting a motor, and a forward part (B) supporting an output shaft (14). A connection device detachably joins the forward housing part (B) and the main housing part (A) and comprises a retaining sleeve (22) which is axially locked to the main housing part (A) by its rear end and extends through the forward housing part (B). The retaining sleeve (22) has two or more threaded axial extensions (28, 29, 30) at its forward end (23), and the forward housing part (B) has an end wall (18) with two or more arc-shaped apertures (33, 34, 35), wherein the threaded extensions (28, 29, 30) are arranged to protrude out through the apertures (33, 34, 35) and hence the end wall (18) to be engaged by a nut (38) for clamping the forward housing part (B) to the main housing part (A).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A portable and handheld power tool comprising a housing (10), a motor, an output shaft (14), and a power transmission (15) coupling the motor to the output shaft (14), wherein the housing (10) comprises a main part (A) supporting the motor and a forward part (B) supporting the output shaft (14), wherein the forward housing part (B) has a front end (23) and a rear end (24), and a connection device detachably joining the forward housing part (B) and the main housing part (A) , c h a r a c t e r i z e d in that â¢ said connection device comprises a retaining sleeve (22) which extends through the forward housing part (B) and has a forward end (23) and a rear end (24), wherein said retaining sleeve (22) is axially locked by said rear end (24) to the main housing part (A), â¢ said retaining sleeve (22) is arranged to extend through the forward housing part (B) in a coaxial disposition relative to the output shaft (14), â¢ said retaining sleeve (22) is formed at its forward end (23) with two or more threaded axial extensions (28), and the forward housing part (B) is formed with two or more apertures (33), wherein said threaded extensions (28) are arranged to extend out of the forward housing part (B) via said apertures (33), and â¢ a nut (38) is arranged to engage said two or more threaded extensions (28) to exert an axially directed clamp force on the forward housing part (12) . 2. A portable and handheld power tool according to claim 1, wherein said retaining sleeve (22) is rotation symmetric and extends through the forward housing part (B) in a coaxial disposition relative to the output shaft (14) . 3. A portable and handheld power tool according to claim 1 or 2, wherein said retaining sleeve (22) is provided at its rear end (24) with a mounting thread (25) arranged to engage a mating mounting thread (26) in the main housing part (A) to 

provide said axial locking of said retaining sleeve (22) to the main housing part (A) . 4. A portable and handheld power tool according to anyone of claims 1-3, wherein said extensions (28) are part cylindrical in shape and located at equal angular intervals over the periphery of the retaining sleeve (22), and said apertures (33) are arc-shaped and arranged to receive said extensions (28) at mounting of the forward housing part (B) on the main housing part (A) . 5. A portable and handheld power tool according to anyone of claims 1-4, wherein the forward housing part (B) has an irregular rear section which is arranged to engage a correspondingly shaped section on the main housing part (A) thereby defining a certain angular position of the forward housing part (B) relative to the main housing part (A) , and the external mounting thread (25) on retaining sleeve (22) in cooperation with the internal mounting thread (26) on the main housing part (A) allows an angular adjustment of the retaining sleeve (22) to make the angular position of said extensions (28) coincide with said apertures (33) on the forward housing part (B) .
